description:
GOAL: Land JIT codegen for the higher-order vec builtins vec_map(v, f) and vec_reduce(v, init, f) so existing fixtures (e.g. ~v[1,2,3,4,5] |> vec_reduce(0, fn (a,b) -> a + b)) run end-to-end through ir_codegen rather than the interp.

## Why a separate ticket

Splits cleanly off fz-ul4.11.14. The vec storage / sigil-alloc / vec_get path is small and elegant; the higher-order ops require a runtime->JIT seam that doesn't exist anywhere else in the codebase yet.

## The architectural seam

vec_map/vec_reduce take a closure as one of their args. To run them via JIT, a runtime fn must invoke a JIT'd closure on each element. Today the trampoline is the only thing that drives compiled fns. Two routes:

- (A) Synthesize a per-call continuation that halts on completion: alloc a sentinel cont frame whose schema points at a special collect-result fn that writes the result to a TLS slot and halts. After fz_closure_invoke, run the trampoline until halt, read the slot.
- (B) Refactor CompiledModule::run to take an entry frame; runtime fn calls run with the closure-invocation frame, recovers the halt value. Cleaner if CompiledModule reachable from runtime fns (TLS handle).

Resolve (A) vs (B) during implementation.

## Deliverables

- Add vec_get / vec_map / vec_reduce to BuiltinTable so ir_lower routes them through Prim::Builtin.
- Codegen for Prim::Builtin variants targeting these three.
- Runtime fns: fz_vec_get (trivial), fz_vec_map (re-entry), fz_vec_reduce (re-entry).

## Tests

- vec_map(double, ~v[1, 2, 3]) -> ~v[2, 4, 6] via JIT.
- vec_reduce(~v[1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 0, fn (a, b) -> a + b) -> 15 via JIT.
- main.rs SAMPLE constant vec_reduce / vec_map calls work via fz run.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.14 (Vec storage + per-kind alloc paths must exist). Closures (.11.19) already landed.

## Out of scope

- VecF64 closure ops (depends on .11.23).
- SIMD codegen (fz-ul4.14).
title: vec_map / vec_reduce via JIT (closure-taking builtins)
